_description: Natural language descriptions of parkour tricks for CLIP-based auto-labeling. Each trick has multiple descriptions to improve matching accuracy. Write descriptions as if explaining to someone who has never seen the trick.
back_flip: a person jumping and rotating backward in the air, landing on their feet
backward somersault with knees tucked to chest during rotation
standing or running backflip, one complete backward rotation
front_flip: a person jumping and rotating forward in the air, landing on their feet
forward somersault with body tucked during rotation
a front flip with forward rotation, one complete somersault forward
side_flip: a person flipping sideways through the air, lateral rotation
sideways somersault with body rotating along the lateral axis
a side flip or side somersault, body rotating sideways
double_full: a person doing a backflip with two complete twists along the body axis
backward somersault with a double twist, the body spins twice while flipping
backflip with two full twists, rotating backward and spinning simultaneously
triple_full: a person doing a backflip with three complete twists along the body axis
backward somersault with a triple twist, extremely fast spinning during the flip
backflip with three full twists, advanced acrobatic trick with rapid rotation
back_full: a person doing a backflip with one complete twist along the body axis
backward somersault with a single full twist, body spins once while flipping backward
a back full twist, one rotation backward with one complete body twist
double_cork: a person performing two off-axis corkscrew rotations in the air
double corkscrew flip, body rotates in a tilted spiral pattern twice
two cork rotations combining flip and twist in an off-axis pattern
castaway: a person doing a raiz or aerial followed by a backward flip in one sequence
a castaway trick combining a side aerial into a back rotation
an acrobatic combination starting with a side rotation into a back flip
raiz: a person performing a no-handed cartwheel that dips backward, an inverted aerial kick
a raiz or slant gainer, the body goes sideways and backward with legs kicking overhead
an aerial cartwheel that leans backward with a leg whipping over
krok: a person performing a swing through aerial with legs splitting in the air
a krok trick with the body horizontal and legs extended in a split position mid-air
an acrobatic swing-through with horizontal body position and extended legs
cork: a person doing a single off-axis corkscrew rotation in the air
one corkscrew rotation combining a flip with a twist in an off-axis spiral
a cork or corkscrew, the body rotates in a tilted spinning pattern
b_twist: a person performing a horizontal spinning rotation with the body nearly parallel to the ground
a butterfly twist with the body flat and spinning horizontally in the air
b-twist or butterfly twist, horizontal rotation with arms and legs extended
tak_full: a person doing a takeoff into a full twisting backflip from one leg
a cheat setup into a full twist backflip, initiated from a turning takeoff
a tak full combining a cheat takeoff with a back full twist
gainer: a person doing a backflip while moving forward, often from a ledge or platform
a gainer backflip where forward momentum carries the body while rotating backward
backward flip performed while traveling forward, typically from an elevated surface
webster: a person doing a front flip from one leg standing on an elevated edge
a webster front flip initiated by tipping forward off a ledge with one leg
single leg front flip from the edge of a platform or wall
kong_vault: a person diving forward over an obstacle, pushing off with both hands, legs passing through arms
a kong vault or monkey vault over a wall or railing with a forward diving motion
hands placed on obstacle, body dives over with legs passing between the arms
speed_vault: a person placing one hand on an obstacle and vaulting over sideways at speed
a fast sideways vault over a low wall with one hand placement while running
speed vault with one hand on the obstacle, body passes sideways at running speed
precision_jump: a person jumping and landing precisely on a small target like a rail or ledge
a precision jump in parkour, landing exactly on a narrow surface with both feet
a standing or running jump to land precisely on a specific spot
wall_flip: a person running at a wall, planting feet on the wall, then flipping backward off it
a wall flip where the athlete runs up the wall and backflips off
wall-assisted backflip, feet kick off the wall to gain rotation
